Home / Patients / Patient Rights & Responsibilities

Patient Rights & Responsibilities

Your Rights

  • · Be treated with dignity and respect
  • · Fair treatment without discrimination of any kind
  • · Make informed decisions regarding your health care
  • · Refuse treatment and be informed of the consequences
  • · Know the names and roles of all caregivers
  • · Receive information in a language you understand
  • · Access your medical records
  • · Confidentiality of records and communications
  • · Choose another healthcare provider
  • · Receive an explanation of your bill
  • · Appropriate assessment and management of pain
  • · Receive care in a safe environment free from abuse or coercion
  • · Voice concerns and complaints
  • · Give or withhold informed consent

Your Responsibilities

  • · Provide accurate and complete information about your health
  • · Actively participate in decisions regarding your health care
  • · Keep scheduled appointments or call to reschedule
  • · Ask questions when something is unclear
  • · Follow the treatment plan agreed upon with your provider
  • · Treat all staff and other patients with courtesy and respect
  • · Respect the rights, privacy, and property of others
  • · Promptly meet any financial obligation agreed to with CMC

Patient Privacy

We value the privacy of every patient. You have rights regarding how your medical information is used and disclosed.

View Privacy Notice →

For Stockton Residents

Mobile Community Response: In partnership with the City of Stockton, Community Medical Centers offers an alternative to 911 for Stockton citizens in need of behavioral health services. If you or someone you know is experiencing mental distress, please call (833) 311-2273, 7 days a week, 8:00 am to 11:00 pm. This helpline is for non-violent individuals with mild to moderate symptoms.
